title: Golden Brown French Sausage Gratin
description: A cultured and pillowy French dish from Brittany, featuring stout and wood-fired bake, that is FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, cook the French sausage over medium-high heat, browning on all sides, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add the butter and cook the sliced onions until they are caramelized and golden brown.
Add the garlic-infused oil, stout beer, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked sausage, onion mixture, and sliced potatoes.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of potatoes, followed by a layer of the sausage and onion mixture, and finally a layer of gruyere cheese.
Repeat the layering process until all ingredients are used, finishing with a layer of cheese on top.
Bake the gratin in the preheated wood-fired oven for 30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
Remove the gratin from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
